entries.definition:
كتاب العين

عجب

(عدس) العين والدال والسين ليس فيه من اللّغة شيء، لكنّهم يسمُّون الحبَّ المعروفَ عَدَساً. ويقولون: عَدَسْ، زجرٌ للبغال. قال:

نَجوتِ وهذا تحملينَ طليقُ

عَدَسْ ما لِعَبَّادٍ عليك إمارةٌ

وقوله:

*إذا حَمَلْتُ بِزَّتي على عَدَس *

فإنّه يريد البغلة، سمَّاها "عَدَسْ" بزَجْرها.

عَآدِّين ʿāddīn [pl. of act. part. عَادّ ʿādd] one who reckons, one who counts, one who keeps an account (23:113) قَالُوٓا۟ لَبِثْنَا يَوْمًا أَوْ بَعْضَ يَوْمٍ فَسْـَٔلِ ٱلْعَآدِّينَ
they said ‘We stayed a day or a part of a day, but ask those who keep count.’

عَدَد ʿadad 1 [v. n./n.] counting, enumerating; number (10:5) وَقَدَّرَهُۥ مَنَازِلَ لِتَعْلَمُوا۟ عَدَدَ ٱلسِّنِينَ وَٱلْحِسَابَ
and [He] measured phases for it [the moon], so that you could know the number of the years (or, to learn how to count the years) and reckoning 2 [adverbial] in terms of counting *(10:5) سِنِينَ عَدَدًا
a great number of years [lit. years by counting]; (18:11) فَضَرَبْنَا عَلَىٰٓ ءَاذَانِهِمْ فِى ٱلْكَهْفِ سِنِينَ عَدَدًا
so We sealed their ears in the cave for a great number of years.

عِدَّة ʿiddatun [n.] 1 number (18:22) رَّبِّىٓ أَعْلَمُ بِعِدَّتِهِم
my Lord knows best their number 2 a fixed term, a stated period (2:185) وَلِتُكْمِلُوا۟ ٱلْعِدَّةَ
that you may complete the prescribed period 3 [jur.] the waiting period divorced and widowed women have to observe before they can remarry (65:1) إِذَا طَلَّقْتُمُ ٱلنِّسَآءَ فَطَلِّقُوهُنَّ لِعِدَّتِهِنَّ وَأَحْصُوا۟ ٱلْعِدَّةَ
when you intend to divorce women, do so at a time when their prescribed waiting period can properly start and calculate the period carefully.

عُدَّة ʿuddatun [coll. n.] equipment; preparation, provision (9:46) وَلَوْ أَرَادُوا۟ ٱلْخُرُوجَ لَأَعَدُّوا۟ لَهُۥ عُدَّةً
had they desired to go forth, they would have made preparations for it.

مَعْدُود maʿdūd [pass. part., fem. مَعْدُودَة maʿdūdatun, fem. pl. مَعْدُودَات maʿdūdāt] countable, counted, reckoned, specified (11:104) وَمَا نُؤَخِّرُهُۥٓ إِلَّا لِأَجَلٍ مَّعْدُودٍ
We only delay it for a specified period; *(12:20) دَرَٰهِمَ مَعْدُودَةٍ
very little money, a paltry sum [lit. countable dirhams]; (2:203) أَيَّامٍ مَّعْدُودَاتٍ
[lit. specific or, numbered days] the 10th, 11th and 12th of the month of Dhū’l-Hijjat (the first of which is the Day, or the Feast, of the Sacrifice) during which the stoning of the devil and other rituals are performed by the pilgrim.

عَآدِّين ʿāddīn [pl. of act. part. عَادّ ʿādd] one who reckons, one who counts, one who keeps an account (23:113) قَالُوٓا۟ لَبِثْنَا يَوْمًا أَوْ بَعْضَ يَوْمٍ فَسْـَٔلِ ٱلْعَآدِّينَ

my Lord knows best their number 2 a fixed term, a stated period (2:185) وَلِتُكْمِلُوا۟ ٱلْعِدَّةَ
that you may complete the prescribed period 3 [jur.] the waiting period divorced and widowed women have to observe before they can remarry (65:1) إِذَا طَلَّقْتُمُ ٱلنِّسَآءَ فَطَلِّقُوهُنَّ لِعِدَّتِهِنَّ وَأَحْصُوا۟ ٱلْعِدَّةَ

عُدَّة ʿuddatun [coll. n.] equipment; preparation, provision (9:46) وَلَوْ أَرَادُوا۟ ٱلْخُرُوجَ لَأَعَدُّوا۟ لَهُۥ عُدَّةً
had they desired to go forth, they would have made preparations for it.

مَعْدُود maʿdūd [pass. part., fem. مَعْدُودَة maʿdūdatun, fem. pl. مَعْدُودَات maʿdūdāt] countable, counted, reckoned, specified (11:104) وَمَا نُؤَخِّرُهُۥٓ إِلَّا لِأَجَلٍ مَّعْدُودٍ
We only delay it for a specified period; *(12:20) دَرَٰهِمَ مَعْدُودَةٍ
very little money, a paltry sum [lit. countable dirhams]; (2:203) أَيَّامٍ مَّعْدُودَاتٍ
[lit. specific or, numbered days] the 10th, 11th and 12th of the month of Dhū’l-Hijjat (the first of which is the Day, or the Feast, of the Sacrifice) during which the stoning of the devil and other rituals are performed by the pilgrim.

entries.definition:
ʿ–d–s a cry used in urging on a mule, to walk with vigour, to travel far and wide; lentils. Of this root, only عَدَس ʿadas, occurs once in the Qur’an.
605
